ngl.def: 'Not gonna lie' — flags honesty or a mild confession.

iykyk.def: 'If you know, you know' — signals an inside reference.

fr.def: 'For real' — emphasises agreement. 'fr fr' = doubly so.

tfw.def: 'That feeling when' — sets up a relatable scenario.

tbh.def: 'To be honest' — prefaces a candid opinion.

fwiw.def: 'For what it's worth' — softens an opinion.

imo.def: 'In my opinion' — flags a personal take.

irl.def: 'In real life' — offline, as opposed to online.

tldr.def: 'Too long; didn't read' — a one-line summary.

atp.def: 'At this point' — introduces a resigned conclusion.

grwm.def: 'Get ready with me' — a video format.

sdiybt.def: An absurdist SpongeBob-derived phrase whose point is that it means nothing.

goat.def: 'Greatest of all time'. Often the goat emoji 🐐.

jomo.def: 'Joy of missing out' — smug opposite of FOMO.

dm.def: 'Direct message'. 'Slide into the DMs' = flirty private contact.

rt.def: 'Retweet' — now used in speech as 'I agree / same'.

l.def: A 'loss' or failure. 'Take the L' = accept defeat.

gg.def: 'Good game' — said at end of a match; loosely 'it's done'.

afk.def: 'Away from keyboard' — not at the screen; absent.

lowkey.def: Slightly, secretly, 'kind of'. Softens a statement.

unserious.def: Ridiculous, not to be taken seriously.

bet.def: 'Okay / deal / for sure' — an energetic agreement.

slay.def: To do something extremely well, or look amazing. Now slightly fading from peak.

sus.def: Suspicious or shady. Popularised by Among Us.

stan.def: An obsessive superfan. From Eminem's song 'Stan'.

ghosting.def: Cutting off all contact suddenly and without explanation.

situationship.def: A romantic arrangement that refuses to define itself.

the ick.def: Sudden, irreversible feeling of repulsion toward someone you were attracted to.

rent-free.def: 'Living rent-free in my head' — you can't stop thinking about it.

based.def: Confidently yourself and indifferent to others' opinions.

cringe.def: Embarrassing second-hand discomfort. Opposite of based.

hits different.def: Feels uniquely better in this context.

touch grass.def: 'Log off and go outside' — telling someone they're too online.

main character.def: Acting like the protagonist of the world.

side eye.def: A look of suspicion or judgement.

era.def: A self-defined life phase: 'in my villain era'.

cooking.def: Doing something impressively well: 'let him cook'.

ratio.def: When a reply outperforms the post it answers.

ate.def: Did something exceptionally well. 'Ate and left no crumbs'.

fell off.def: Declined in quality or relevance from a former peak.

gaslight.def: Manipulating someone into doubting their own reality.

bussin.def: Extremely good, usually food. Officially graveyarded — using it now reads as out-of-touch.

it's giving.def: 'It's giving [X]' — it has the vibe of X.

pull up.def: To show up or come through.

rizz.def: Charisma — now graveyarded; using it sincerely is a dead giveaway you're a millennial.

delulu.def: 'Delusional', affectionately. The cliché alarm finally went off — sincere usage now reads as dated.

sigma.def: A 'lone wolf' male archetype, now mostly ironic.

skibidi.def: A mood-dependent intensifier from Skibidi Toilet. Officially in the cringe pile now.

yeet.def: To throw something with force. Firmly dated now.

cheugy.def: Off-trend in a millennial way. The word itself has become cheugy.

mewing.def: Now meme shorthand for staying silent. 'I'm just mewing'.

crash out.def: To lose emotional control spectacularly. Defining term of 2025-26.

choppelganger.def: 'Chopped' + 'doppelganger' — an unflattering lookalike. The format is dead; the word is going with it.

clanker.def: Derogatory term for AI, robots, or AI-reliant people.

ai slop.def: Low-effort, mass-produced AI content flooding feeds.

shrekking.def: Dating someone you're not attracted to hoping they'll treat you well. Now cringe — the discourse moved on.

i know ball.def: 'I deeply understand this and have elite taste.'

404 coded.def: Acting absent, empty, or error-like. Archived — the -coded family kept going without it.

beige flag.def: A partner trait that's neither good nor bad, just oddly boring.

brain rot.def: The mushy-brain feeling from too much absurd internet content. Oxford WOTY 2024.

aura farming.def: Sincere usage is officially graveyarded — what survives is the joke.

six seven.def: '6-7' — nonsense audio meme. Officially graveyarded in middle schools.

looksmaxxing.def: Peak-phase obsession now graveyarded; the manosphere has moved on.

mogging.def: Visibly outshining someone in looks or presence.

sneaky link.def: A secret romantic meetup, or the person you're secretly seeing.

body count.def: The number of people someone has slept with.

thirst trap.def: A deliberately attractive photo posted for attention.

ragebait.def: Content posted purely to provoke angry reactions.

edgelord.def: Someone who performs shock value for attention.

micro-drama.def: Ultra-short serialized vertical video soap operas.

great meme reset.def: A 2026 movement calling for a return to nostalgic, human-made memes.

nihilistic penguin.def: A penguin from a 2007 documentary marching alone toward mountains — 2026 mascot of opting out.

kitkat heist.def: Memes about the real 2026 theft of ~400,000 KitKat bars.

i am my perimeter.def: A streamer's deadpan R6 line turned all-purpose 'locked in' declaration.

material lover.def: Soundtrack reference to Sienna Spiro's Devil Wears Prada 2 song — fashion/aspirational audio. Confirmed in a Target ad by late June, officially retired.

remember who you are.def: Reels caption format over 'Freakin' Out' — travel, training, glow-up montages.

saxophone gets louder.def: Audio format where sax music swells as on-screen chaos collapses.

banger badge.def: X's 'Certified Banger' badge — became a meme within hours, faded almost as fast.

analog posting.def: Posting deliberately low-tech content as anti-AI-slop signalling.

i have therapy.def: Reels POV format that cuts from 'sorry, I have therapy' to the creator's actual coping ritual. Wholesome flex.

homer drops his donut.def: AI-generated meme song where Homer 'D'oh's' over every micro-defeat. The slop the Reset crowd grudgingly liked — usurped by Withered Flight by late June.

red vs blue button.def: Viral two-button moral-dilemma format. Every niche now has its own version; the debates outlive the prompts.

algorithm prefers.def: A/B-style format pitting a high-effort edit against a deadpan static shot, letting the comments vote which wins.

game time.def: Future × Tyla FIFA World Cup 2026 audio — sports edits, gym, travel, World Cup anticipation.

red card confusion.def: The South Africa bench's bewildered reactions to three red cards in the Mexico vs. SA World Cup 2026 opener — first viral meme of the tournament.
red card confusion.origin: FIFA WC 2026 opener at Azteca Stadium, June 12, 2026

sad for a girl so in love.def: Lyric from Olivia Rodrigo's June 12 2026 album, used as a caption template for emotional/situationship posts.

unc.def: Short for 'uncle' — affectionate for an older man, or teasing someone for being out of touch.

pop off.def: To do something exceptionally well, or to hype someone up for doing so. 'Pop off, king.'

dustin lucas will mike.def: Stranger Things cast's Jimmy Fallon rap recap, turned into a TikTok exploitable in June 2026 — usually paired with the Withered Flight visual.
dustin lucas will mike.origin: Tonight Show w/ Jimmy Fallon, late 2025 → TikTok, June 2026

withered flight.def: Analog-horror style image macro using a distorted edit of YouTuber FlightReacts. Pairs with the Dustin Lucas Will Mike audio.

rock music.def: Charli XCX's 'Rock Music' track powering the stuck-frame glitch edit format on TikTok in mid-2026.
rock music.origin: Charli XCX track → TikTok glitch edits, June 2026

petty mode.def: Acting out small spiteful behaviours on purpose, usually played for comedy. 'In my petty era' adjacent.

hobby dogging.def: The act of pretending to walk a dog as a casual hobby, often done for fun or comedic effect.
hobby dogging.origin: Originated in Germany, gaining popularity as a viral trend where participants engage in humorous or absurd activities while walking their dogs.

reverse location search.def: A trend where users utilize AI to identify locations based on images they post, effectively flipping traditional location tagging.
reverse location search.origin: Emerging from viral ChatGPT trends, highlighting the capabilities of AI in interpreting visual content.

chimpfluencers.def: Individuals who gain popularity on social media platforms by sharing humorous or absurd content involving animals, particularly showcasing them in unconventional situations.
chimpfluencers.origin: A blend of 'chimp' and 'influencers', reflecting the trend of using animal antics to engage audiences.

grass sticking.def: A humorous act of pretending to be unaware or oblivious, often by physically sticking grass in one's ears, as a metaphor for ignoring reality.
grass sticking.origin: Derived from the viral trend where people mimic animals by sticking grass in their ears, used to signify disengagement or denial.

viking row.def: A viral trend where participants emulate Viking-style rowing, often used humorously in social media posts to depict teamwork or struggle.
viking row.origin: Originated from social media challenges during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, where fans creatively reenact Viking rowing scenes.

fifa fever.def: A heightened excitement or enthusiasm surrounding the FIFA World Cup, often seen in social media posts or conversations during the tournament.
fifa fever.origin: Emerging during the lead-up to the 2026 FIFA World Cup as fans globally engage with the event.

meme-splosion.def: A sudden and overwhelming surge of memes related to a specific event or trending topic, often flooding social media platforms.
meme-splosion.origin: Coined as a reaction to the rapid spread of memes during significant events like the FIFA World Cup and popular films.

vibe check.def: A casual inquiry into someone's feelings or the general mood of a situation, often used to assess compatibility or energy in social settings.
vibe check.origin: Gained popularity through social media, particularly TikTok, as a way to connect with friends and gauge atmospheres.

goalie goals.def: Referring to impressive actions or achievements by goalkeepers in soccer, often used humorously to highlight unexpected skills or plays.
goalie goals.origin: Popularized by social media highlights during soccer matches, especially during the World Cup.

citizen vigilante.def: A person who takes the law into their own hands to protect their community, often acting outside of official law enforcement channels.
citizen vigilante.origin: Gained popularity in discussions and memes surrounding justice and activism, particularly amidst rising concerns over crime and safety.

reverse chatgpt.def: A trend where users prompt ChatGPT or similar AI to generate outputs based on images or other inputs, effectively reversing the traditional use of AI for text generation.
reverse chatgpt.origin: Originated from the trend of using AI in creative ways, evolving from standard text prompts to more visual and interactive formats.

live-updating meme.def: A meme format that evolves in real-time, allowing users to adapt and remix content as events unfold or trends change.
live-updating meme.origin: Gained popularity as social media platforms encouraged more dynamic and engaging content sharing.

world cup fever.def: A heightened excitement and anticipation surrounding the FIFA World Cup, often leading to intense discussions, gatherings, and social media engagement related to the event.
world cup fever.origin: Coined during the lead-up to the 2026 FIFA World Cup as fans and players ramp up their enthusiasm for the tournament.

gen z slang revolution.def: The movement towards using and popularizing unique slang terms and phrases among Gen Z, often characterized by creativity and social media influence.
gen z slang revolution.origin: Emerged as a reaction to traditional language norms and is fueled by platforms like TikTok and Twitter.

what a week, huh?.def: An expression used to summarize the chaotic events of a week, often accompanied by humorous or relatable images.
what a week, huh?.origin: Evolved from social media reactions to weekly news cycles, gaining traction on platforms like TikTok.

fifa world cup meme mashup.def: A viral trend combining FIFA World Cup highlights with humorous edits, often featuring unexpected pop culture references.
fifa world cup meme mashup.origin: Emerged during the 2026 FIFA World Cup as fans creatively remixed game footage with popular memes.

hobby dogging 2.0.def: A playful trend where participants pretend to engage in dog-walking activities in unusual or humorous settings.
hobby dogging 2.0.origin: A continuation of the original hobby dogging trend, gaining traction in online communities for its absurdity.

cape verde culture surge.def: A significant increase in global interest and appreciation for Cape Verdean culture, including music, art, and cuisine, often fueled by social media.
cape verde culture surge.origin: Emerging from the rising popularity of Cape Verdean artists and cultural events, particularly around the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

2026 fifa world cup parody challenges.def: Humorous challenges and skits that parody the events, players, or controversies surrounding the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
2026 fifa world cup parody challenges.origin: Spurred by the excitement and memes related to the World Cup, with users creating original content that satirizes the tournament.

kylian apology challenge.def: A viral trend where fans create humorous video apologies to Kylian Mbappé for perceived slights or criticisms, often using exaggerated expressions of remorse.
kylian apology challenge.origin: Emerging from a petition by French fans seeking forgiveness from Mbappé after a controversial match.

alpha film meme mashup.def: A creative trend where clips or images from the film 'Alpha' are humorously combined with popular memes to create relatable or absurd content.
alpha film meme mashup.origin: Stemming from the popularity of the film 'Alpha' and its integration into the meme culture on platforms like TikTok.

soccer star aesthetic.def: A style or vibe characterized by a blend of athleticism and fashion, often inspired by popular soccer players and their off-field looks.
soccer star aesthetic.origin: Emerging from social media trends where Gen Z showcases fashion influenced by soccer culture and prominent players.

fifa world cup meme challenge.def: A viral social media challenge where participants create humorous memes related to the FIFA World Cup, often featuring famous players or memorable moments.
fifa world cup meme challenge.origin: Emerging during the intense discussions and excitement surrounding the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

erling haaland rap challenge.def: A viral trend where fans create rap songs or challenge others to rap about Erling Haaland, often highlighting his achievements in football.
erling haaland rap challenge.origin: Originated from social media platforms as a playful way to celebrate the football star's rising fame during the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

needohs trend.def: Refers to the popular activity of using NeeDoh stress balls as a fidget toy during stressful situations, often shared in videos and memes.
needohs trend.origin: Gained traction on TikTok as users showcased the satisfying experience of squishing and playing with NeeDohs.

world cup wonderwall.def: A viral trend celebrating iconic moments and players from the FIFA World Cup, often accompanied by humorous or nostalgic commentary.
world cup wonderwall.origin: The term emerged during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, referencing the popular song 'Wonderwall' and its cultural significance.

erling haaland sexy pics trend.def: A trend where fans share and discuss attractive photos of footballer Erling Haaland, often highlighting his appeal both on and off the field.
erling haaland sexy pics trend.origin: The trend gained traction during the 2026 FIFA World Cup, fueled by social media and fan engagement.

evil dead burn challenge.def: A viral challenge where participants film themselves creating humorous and exaggerated versions of horror scenes, inspired by the 'Evil Dead' franchise.
evil dead burn challenge.origin: The trend emerged following the release of the latest 'Evil Dead' film, encouraging fans to creatively engage with the franchise.

bonnie tyler nostalgia wave.def: A cultural moment characterized by a resurgence of interest in the music and themes from Bonnie Tyler’s hits, particularly in nostalgic media and social platforms.
bonnie tyler nostalgia wave.origin: This wave gained traction as her songs were featured in various memes and nostalgic retrospectives on platforms like TikTok.

fifa parody challenge.def: A viral trend where participants create humorous or satirical content related to the 2026 FIFA World Cup, often mimicking famous moments or players in a comedic light.
fifa parody challenge.origin: Emerging from the excitement surrounding the FIFA World Cup 2026, these parodies capitalize on the global passion for soccer.

reverse chatgpt prompt.def: A trend where users ask ChatGPT to generate content based on specific outcomes or responses they desire, flipping the traditional prompting model.
reverse chatgpt prompt.origin: Gained popularity as users sought to explore more creative and personalized interactions with AI tools.

haaland's highlight reel challenge.def: A viral trend where users create and share compilations of impressive moments or achievements inspired by soccer player Erling Haaland's performance highlights.
haaland's highlight reel challenge.origin: This trend emerged in July 2026, coinciding with Haaland's rising popularity during the FIFA World Cup.

barbie peptide.def: A trendy new supplement that claims to enhance beauty and wellness, inspired by the cultural phenomenon of Barbie aesthetics.
barbie peptide.origin: Emerging from viral discussions on social media about beauty trends and wellness products.

digger transformation challenge.def: A viral social media challenge where participants showcase dramatic physical transformations, inspired by the film 'Digger' and its promotional content.
digger transformation challenge.origin: Originated from the release of the film 'Digger' in 2026, gaining traction through platforms like TikTok.

lamine yamal challenge.def: A viral challenge inspired by Lamine Yamal, encouraging participants to showcase their unique skills or talents in a short video format, often accompanied by a specific soundtrack.
lamine yamal challenge.origin: Emerging from the popularity of young footballer Lamine Yamal, the challenge gained traction on social media platforms like TikTok.

falkland islands meme revival.def: A resurgence of humor and content related to the Falkland Islands, often used to comment on current events or nostalgia.
falkland islands meme revival.origin: Gained popularity through social media platforms as users began to reference the Falkland Islands in comedic or ironic contexts.

soccer star aesthetic revamp.def: A style or trend focused on emulating the fashion and persona of popular soccer players, often incorporating high-end athletic wear and casual chic.
soccer star aesthetic revamp.origin: Emerged alongside the rise of social media influencers and the visibility of soccer stars' personal styles.

💀.def: Skull = 'I'm dead' — something is so funny you've metaphorically died laughing.

😭.def: Rarely actual crying — usually 'this is hilarious'.

🧢.def: The cap = a lie. Reply '🧢' to call cap.

🗿.def: The moai = deadpan, stoic, ironic 'sigma' energy.

🤡.def: The clown = self-own or calling someone foolish.

🐐.def: The goat = GOAT, greatest of all time.

🦀.def: Wilted rose = melodramatic heartbreak or ironic despair.

👁️👄👁️.def: Wide eyes and lips = stunned witnessing of chaos.

fairs.def: From a June 16 2026 @yowheredidjogo TikTok — creators posing for 'fairs' stickers meant to be screenshotted and reused as reactions.

du bist gut genug.def: German for 'you are good enough'. A wholesome audio paired with cartoon mashups (Cleveland Brown Jr. is the breakout) for surreal-emotional comedy.
du bist gut genug.origin: TikTok audio meme → cross-platform, early June 2026

pressure.def: Nyck Caution's track 'PRESSURE!' — dominant audio for World Cup highlights, gym edits, and clutch-moment content in June 2026.
pressure.origin: Nyck Caution single → TikTok WC wave, June 2026

rare aesthetic.def: Nostalgic compilation format pairing oddly-specific or strangely-satisfying clips with the 'rare aesthetic' label.

food jutsu.def: Cooking videos shot as if the food prep were a ninja technique — stylised, dramatic, lightly absurd.

like a prayer.def: Josh Fawaz's remix of Madonna's 'Like a Prayer' — the unofficial 2026 Summer Anthem. 7-second lip-sync + '2026 Summer Anthem' text overlay.

summer anthem.def: The '2026 Summer Anthem' text-overlay format — slap it on a 7-sec clip set to Josh Fawaz's 'Like a Prayer' remix and post.

puerto rico song.def: Saxboy Billy's 'The Puerto Rico Song' — a lip-sync and dance audio that took off for outfit and travel content in June 2026.

same-pose lineup.def: Anime-style product-reveal format: a group strikes the same pose, each holding an object that represents them, in a rapid-fire lineup.

smells like teen spirit.def: Nirvana's 1991 anthem newly viral on the 'oh well, whatever, nevermind' lyric — carefree single-shot shrug edits.

my boyfriend's in tech.def: Photo-carousel format set to Meg Myers' 'Desire': a status-flex intro that flips to a sincere third slide defending the loved one's work.

desire.def: Meg Myers' 'Desire' as the audio behind the 'my boyfriend's in tech' honest-carousel trend.
desire.origin: Meg Myers track → TikTok carousels, June 2026

pageText: ngl the dustin lucas will mike audio is doing numbers — paired with withered flight it's the format of the week 💀. meanwhile HOTD S3 dropped today so every Targaryen edit is set to PRESSURE!, and du bist gut genug is still rewiring brain chemistry. clanker accounts still posting ai slop tho, istg the great meme reset already won. atp the nihilistic penguin understood the assignment — he saw the discourse and chose the mountains, and tbh? based.
examples: dustin lucas will mike + withered flight is the cinematic universe we earned
HOTD S3 just dropped and every dragon-fire edit is PRESSURE!-coded, no notes
fairs format was born jun 16, eaten jun 18, brand-coopted jun 20 — textbook 2026 lifecycle
